member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Alpaca.AlpacaBrokerageGateway
member.summary: Alpaca brokerage gateway for live order execution via the Alpaca Trading API. Uses REST API for order management and a bounded channel for execution report streaming.
member.remarks: Alpaca Trading API v2: - Paper trading: paper-api.alpaca.markets - Live trading: api.alpaca.markets - Supports market, limit, stop, stop-limit orders - Supports fractional shares - Supports extended hours trading - Rate limit: 200 req/min

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Alpaca.AlpacaHistoricalDataProvider
member.summary: Historical data provider using Alpaca Markets Data API v2. Provides daily and intraday OHLCV bars with split/dividend adjustments, as well as tick-level quotes (NBBO), trades, and auction data. Coverage: US equities, ETFs. Extends BaseHistoricalDataProvider for common functionality including: - HTTP resilience (retry, circuit breaker) - Rate limit tracking with IRateLimitAwareProvider - Centralized error handling

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Alpaca.AlpacaHistoricalDataProvider.Capabilities
member.summary: Alpaca supports all data types: adjusted bars, intraday, quotes, trades, and auctions.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Alpaca.AlpacaMarketDataClient
member.summary:
Alpaca Market Data client (WebSocket) that implements the IMarketDataClient abstraction. Extends , which centralises connection lifecycle, resilience (retry + circuit breaker), heartbeat monitoring, and automatic reconnection. ~80 LOC of WebSocket boilerplate removed compared to the previous direct implementation.
Current support: - Trades: YES (streams "t" messages and forwards to TradeDataCollector) - Depth (L2): NO (Alpaca stock stream provides quotes/BBO, not full L2 updates; method returns -1)
Notes: - Alpaca typically limits to 1 active stream connection per user per endpoint. - Authentication is performed by sending an "auth" message immediately after connect. The auth response arrives in the main receive loop (no separate handshake step).

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Alpaca.AlpacaMarketDataClient.AuthenticateAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.remarks: Alpaca does not use a pre-loop handshake. Authentication is performed by sending a single "auth" message; the auth success/failure response arrives in as a status message (type "success" or "error").

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Alpaca.AlpacaOptionsChainProvider
member.summary: Options chain provider using the Alpaca Markets Data API v1beta1. Provides option chain snapshots, greeks, and contract discovery for US equity options. Requires a funded Alpaca brokerage account with options trading enabled.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Alpaca.AlpacaOptionsChainProvider.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Net.Http.IHttpClientFactory,System.Net.Http.HttpClient,Serilog.ILogger)
member.summary: Creates a new Alpaca options chain provider.
member.param: Alpaca API key ID (falls back to ALPACA_KEY_ID env var).
Alpaca API secret key (falls back to ALPACA_SECRET_KEY env var).
Optional to create the underlying HTTP client (ADR-010). When provided, a named client "alpaca-options" is created so shared resilience policies and handler lifetimes are centrally managed.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Alpaca.AlpacaProviderModule
member.summary: Provider module for Alpaca Markets. Registers all four Alpaca capability types — streaming, historical backfill, symbol search, and brokerage — in a single DI call.
member.remarks:
This is the first module implementation and serves as the migration target for the Alpaca adapter family (currently also wired individually in ProviderFeatureRegistration and ProviderFactory). Once this module is used as the primary registration path, the per-capability wiring in those classes can be removed incrementally.
Credential resolution falls back to environment variables at construction time (ALPACA_KEY_ID, ALPACA_SECRET_KEY), so the module registers even when credentials are not yet configured; providers self-report as unavailable via .

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Alpaca.AlpacaProviderModule.ValidateAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Validation always passes: providers handle missing credentials gracefully at runtime (returning empty data or reporting themselves as unavailable).

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Alpaca.AlpacaSymbolSearchProviderRefactored
member.summary: Refactored symbol search provider using Alpaca Markets API, now extending BaseSymbolSearchProvider. Provides asset search for US equities with trading status information.
member.remarks:
This refactored version eliminates ~150 lines of boilerplate code by leveraging the BaseSymbolSearchProvider abstract class for common functionality.
Key differences from original: - Uses base class for HTTP client, rate limiting, and disposal - Cleaner separation between API-specific and common logic - Consistent error handling via base class

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Alpaca.AlpacaSymbolSearchProviderRefactored.ApplyFilters(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Models.SymbolSearchResult},System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Alpaca supports filtering natively via URL parameters, so we skip client-side filtering.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.AlphaVantage.AlphaVantageHistoricalDataProvider
member.summary: Historical data provider using Alpha Vantage API (free tier with API key). Unique capability: Intraday historical data (1, 5, 15, 30, 60 min intervals). Coverage: US equities, global indices, forex, crypto. Free tier: 25 requests/day (severely limited), 5 calls/minute. Extends BaseHistoricalDataProvider for common functionality.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.BaseHistoricalDataProvider
member.summary: Base class for historical data providers to eliminate duplicate code patterns across all backfill provider implementations. Provides common functionality for: - Rate limiting initialization and management - HTTP client setup and error handling - HTTP resilience (retry, circuit breaker, timeout) - Credential validation - Symbol normalization - Disposed state tracking
member.remarks: Derived classes inherit: - implementation with virtual event - Centralized HTTP error handling via - HTTP resilience pipeline for transient fault handling - Rate limit tracking and Retry-After extraction

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.BaseSymbolSearchProvider
member.summary: Base class for symbol search providers that eliminates common boilerplate code. Provides standardized handling of: - HTTP client initialization via factory pattern - Rate limiting with configurable limits - Credential validation and header setup - Disposal pattern - Error handling and logging - Match score calculation
member.remarks: Derived classes should override abstract properties and implement the protected abstract methods for provider-specific API integration.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.CompositeProviderOptions.RateLimitRotationThreshold
member.summary: Threshold (0.0 to 1.0) at which a provider is considered "approaching" its rate limit. Providers exceeding this threshold will be deprioritized in favor of providers with more capacity. Default: 0.8 (80% of rate limit used).

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.ICorporateActionProvider
member.summary: Provider-agnostic interface for fetching corporate action history (dividends and stock splits) from a data source and merging it into the Security Master event store.

remarks.para: Implementations should be decorated with so they are discoverable via the attribute-based provider registry (ADR-005). The Security Master aggregates results from all registered implementations with last-writer-wins conflict resolution keyed by (securityId, ex_date, corporateActionType).
Implementations must degrade gracefully (log and return empty) when the upstream API is unavailable or the ticker is not recognised by the provider.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.ICorporateActionProvider.ProviderId
member.summary: Returns the provider identifier string used to tag corporate action events when multiple sources contribute data for the same security.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.ICorporateActionProvider.FetchAsync(System.String,System.Guid,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Fetches all available corporate actions (dividends, splits, mergers, etc.) for and from the upstream data source.
member.param: The Security Master identifier for the security.
member.returns: Zero or more objects ready to be dispatched to the Security Master event store. The caller is responsible for deduplication.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.CorporateActionCommand

Command model produced by an implementation. Contains the fields needed to raise a CorporateActionDeclared domain event.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.IHistoricalDataProvider
member.summary: Unified contract for fetching historical data from vendors. Consolidates previous V1, V2, and Extended interfaces into a single contract with optional capabilities indicated by properties.
member.remarks:
This interface is the core contract for ADR-001 (Provider Abstraction Pattern). All historical data providers must implement this interface for backfill operations.
Capabilities are indicated via properties (SupportsXxx) rather than interface inheritance, allowing consumers to check capabilities without type casting.
Implements for unified provider discovery and capability reporting across all provider types.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.IHistoricalDataProvider.RateLimitDelay
member.summary: Minimum delay between API calls to respect rate limits. Default: no delay.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.ProviderBehaviorBuilder
member.summary: Fluent builder that produces a fully functional from delegate functions, without requiring inheritance from .
member.remarks:
Use this builder for new provider implementations where the full HTTP/rate-limit infrastructure of is not needed, or when wrapping a third-party SDK that manages its own HTTP layer.
Existing providers built on remain unchanged until the module migration is complete; the builder is the preferred path for any provider added from this point onward.
Minimal example:
IHistoricalDataProvider provider = ProviderBehaviorBuilder.Create() .WithName("my-provider") .WithDisplayName("My Provider") .WithCapabilities(HistoricalDataCapabilities.BarsOnly) .WithDailyBars(async (symbol, from, to, ct) => { var bars = await _sdk.FetchBarsAsync(symbol, from, to, ct); return bars.Select(MapBar).ToList(); }) .Build();

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.ProviderSubscriptionRanges
member.summary:
Centralized subscription ID ranges for each provider to prevent collisions.
Each provider allocates subscription IDs from a designated range to ensure unique IDs across the application. This prevents confusion when debugging subscription issues and ensures proper unsubscription.
Range allocation: - 100,000-199,999: Alpaca Markets - 200,000-299,999: Polygon.io - 300,000-399,999: StockSharp - 400,000-499,999: Interactive Brokers - 500,000-599,999: NYSE - 600,000+: Reserved for future providers
Usage:
private readonly SubscriptionManager _subscriptionManager = new(startingId: ProviderSubscriptionRanges.AlpacaStart);

member.name: F:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.ProviderSubscriptionRanges.AlpacaStart

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.WebSocketProviderBase
member.summary:
Abstract base class for WebSocket-based streaming market data providers. Consolidates connection lifecycle, heartbeat monitoring, and automatic reconnection logic that was previously duplicated across Alpaca, Polygon, NYSE, and StockSharp.
Derived classes implement protocol-specific hooks:
— send provider-specific auth messages
— parse and route incoming messages
— re-subscribe symbols after reconnection
— construct the provider-specific endpoint URI
— set provider-specific headers/options
member.remarks: Connection management is delegated to which provides resilience (retry + circuit breaker), heartbeat monitoring, and gated reconnection to prevent reconnection storms.

member.name: F:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Core.WebSocketProviderBase.Log
member.summary: Logger instance for the derived provider.

member.summary: Creates a new WebSocket provider base.
member.param: Starting subscription ID for this provider's range.

member.summary: Builds the WebSocket endpoint URI for this provider.

member.summary: Sends provider-specific authentication messages after WebSocket connection.

member.param: The raw text message from the WebSocket.

member.summary: Optionally configures the before connecting (e.g., setting authorization headers, sub-protocols). Default implementation does nothing.

member.summary: Sends a text message through the WebSocket connection.

member.summary: Reads a single text message directly from the WebSocket. Call only during — before the receive loop starts — to handle initial handshake messages (e.g., connection confirmation, auth response).

member.summary: Records heartbeat activity. Call this when data is received to prevent stale connection detection.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Edgar.EdgarSecurityMasterIngestProvider
member.summary: Pages through the SEC EDGAR company_tickers.json endpoint and optionally enriches each record with submission-level metadata (SIC code, exchanges, state of incorporation) by fetching individual /submissions/CIK{cik}.json records. Yields objects ready for Security Master bulk ingest.
member.remarks: No API key is required — the SEC EDGAR API is freely accessible. The SEC asks callers to include a User-Agent header identifying the application and to respect the 10-request-per-second rate limit. See https://www.sec.gov/developer for guidance.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Edgar.EdgarSecurityMasterIngestProvider.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Edgar.EdgarSecurityMasterIngestProvider},System.Net.Http.HttpClient,System.Net.Http.HttpClient)
member.param: Optional HTTP client aimed at www.sec.gov. A new instance is created if null.
Optional HTTP client aimed at data.sec.gov. A new instance is created if null.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Edgar.EdgarSecurityMasterIngestProvider.FetchAllAsync(System.Boolean,System.IProgress{System.Int32},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Fetches all companies from EDGAR and returns them as Security Master create-requests.
member.param: When true, each company's /submissions/CIK*.json record is fetched to populate SIC code, exchanges, and state-of-incorporation. This is much slower (one extra HTTP call per company) and is disabled by default.
Optional progress callback invoked after each processed page.
member.returns: All matching companies as create-security requests.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Edgar.EdgarSecurityMasterIngestProvider.ParseCompanyTickers(System.String)
member.summary: Parses the dictionary-of-objects format returned by company_tickers.json.

member.summary: Maps an EDGAR SIC code range (or filer category) to a canonical asset class. SIC codes: see EDGAR company search at www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Edgar.EdgarSymbolSearchProvider
member.summary: Symbol search provider backed by the SEC EDGAR public API. Uses the free /files/company_tickers.json endpoint — no API key required. The full company list (~13 000 entries) is fetched once and cached in-memory for the lifetime of the provider to stay well within the SEC's 10-request-per-second guidance.
member.remarks: SEC EDGAR API reference: https://www.sec.gov/developer Polite-use requirements: include a User-Agent header identifying the application.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Failover.FailoverAwareMarketDataClient
member.summary: A composite that wraps multiple provider clients and automatically switches between them based on failover rules managed by .
member.remarks: This client is transparent to callers — it implements and delegates to whichever provider is currently active. When a failover event occurs, it disconnects the failed provider, connects the new one, and re-subscribes all active symbols.

member.summary: Creates a new failover-aware client.
member.param: Map of provider ID to client instance.
The failover rule ID this client corresponds to.
The provider ID to start with (typically the primary).

member.summary: Singleton registry that holds the runtime instance. Allows API endpoints (in the Ui.Shared layer) to access the failover service without requiring a direct project reference to the runtime.
member.remarks: Register this as a singleton in DI. Set from Program.cs after creating the failover service. API endpoints can then resolve it to query live failover state and trigger manual failovers.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Failover.StreamingFailoverService
member.summary: Orchestrates automatic failover between streaming market data providers. Monitors provider health via , evaluates configured rules, and triggers switchover when consecutive failures exceed the threshold.
member.remarks: Implements ADR-001 provider abstraction with runtime failover coordination that was previously missing for streaming providers.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.InteractiveBrokers.ContractFactory.ThrowPlatformNotSupported
member.summary: Centralizes the non-IBAPI guard so every conditional-compilation stub throws the same guidance.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.InteractiveBrokers.EnhancedIBConnectionManager
member.summary:
IB connection manager that owns the socket/EReader loop and forwards raw callbacks into .
This file is buildable out-of-the-box: - When compiled WITHOUT the official IB API reference, it exposes a small stub implementation. - When compiled WITH the IB API (define the compilation constant IBAPI and reference IBApi), it provides a full EWrapper implementation with depth routing.
Connection Resilience (IBAPI build): - Uses ExponentialBackoffRetry for connection retry with configurable delays (1s initial, 2min max) - Implements HeartbeatMonitor for connection health detection - Automatic reconnection on connection loss with jitter to prevent thundering herd - Unlimited retry attempts by default (configurable via maxRetries parameter) - See EnhancedIBConnectionManager.IBApi.cs and Infrastructure/Performance/ConnectionWarmUp.cs for implementation

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.InteractiveBrokers.EnhancedIBConnectionManager.ThrowPlatformNotSupported
member.summary: Centralizes the platform guard used by the non-IBAPI build so each stub entry point stays concise.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.InteractiveBrokers.IBApiVersionValidator
member.summary: Validates the IB API server and client library versions at startup. Throws when the connected TWS/Gateway server version is below the minimum required by Meridian, and logs a warning when the server version exceeds the highest version tested in CI.
member.remarks:
Version numbers used here are IB EClient/EWrapper "server version" integers exchanged during the initial TCP handshake (EClientSocket.ServerVersion after Connect). They are NOT the same as the human-readable TWS release numbers shown in the TWS title bar (e.g. "10.19").
Meridian version compatibility:
Server version < 70Not supported — missing reqTickByTickData, reqSmartComponents, and other modern API methods.
70 – 178Supported and tested.
> 178Untested — Meridian will continue but log a warning.

member.name: F:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.InteractiveBrokers.IBApiVersionValidator.MinSupportedServerVersion
member.summary: Minimum IB server version Meridian requires. Introduced with TWS 966+. Below this version, reqTickByTickData and other critical methods are unavailable.

member.name: F:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.InteractiveBrokers.IBApiVersionValidator.MaxTestedServerVersion
member.summary: The highest IB server version verified in Meridian CI. Server versions above this are accepted but trigger a warning so operators can update this constant after confirming compatibility.

member.name: F:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.InteractiveBrokers.IBApiVersionValidator.MinSupportedClientVersion
member.summary: Minimum IB client API library version (IBApi DLL) that Meridian is built against. This maps to the TWS API installer version 10.19.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.InteractiveBrokers.IBApiVersionValidator.ValidateServerVersion(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Validates the returned by TWS/Gateway after connection. Call this immediately after EClientSocket.Connect succeeds.
member.param: The integer server version from EClientSocket.ServerVersion.
The integer client version from the IBApi DLL (pass EClient.MIN_VERSION or the constant defined in the official SDK).

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.InteractiveBrokers.IBApiVersionValidator.BuildVersionRequirementsMessage
member.summary: Returns a human-readable summary of the version requirements, suitable for inclusion in startup logs or operator runbooks.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.InteractiveBrokers.IBBrokerageGateway
member.summary: Interactive Brokers brokerage gateway for live order execution via the TWS/Gateway API. Communicates with IB TWS or IB Gateway running locally. Supports equities, options, futures, and forex.
member.remarks:
IB TWS API: - Connects to TWS or IB Gateway on localhost (default port 7496 live, 7497 paper) - Full order lifecycle: submit, modify, cancel with real-time execution reports - Supports market, limit, stop, stop-limit, and many more order types - Supports partial fills and order modification - Rate limit: 50 messages/sec
This implementation provides the brokerage abstraction layer. The actual TWS API communication is delegated to the IB connection infrastructure. When IBAPI is not compiled in, this gateway operates in simulation mode using synthetic responses.

member.summary: Handles an IB tickOptionComputation callback (tickType 10=Bid, 11=Ask, 12=Last, 13=Model). Only model ticks (tickType == 13) are used to populate greeks — bid/ask/last option computation ticks use the same IB callback but carry less reliable IV/greeks values.
member.param: The IB request id that identifies the option contract.
Implied volatility (IB transmits -1 when not available).
Option delta (-1 to +1; IB transmits -2 when not available).
Option price; IB transmits -1 when not available.
Present value of dividends expected before expiry.
Option gamma; IB transmits -2 when not available.
Option vega; IB transmits -2 when not available.
Option theta; IB transmits -2 when not available.
Underlying price at time of computation; IB transmits -1 when not available.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.NYSE.NYSEDataSource
member.summary:
NYSE Direct Connection data source providing real-time and historical market data directly from the New York Stock Exchange.
Features: - Real-time trades and quotes via NYSE Integrated Feed - Historical daily OHLCV bars via NYSE Historical Data API - Level 2 market depth (Premium/Professional tiers) - Trade conditions and participant IDs - Pre-market and after-hours data - Corporate actions (dividends, splits)
Requires NYSE Connect API credentials and appropriate data subscriptions.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.NYSE.NyseNationalTradesCsvParser
member.summary:
Parser for NYSE TAQ (Trade and Quote) National Trades CSV files.
File format: EQY_US_TAQ_NATIONAL_TRADES_YYYYMMDD.gz Distributed via NYSE FTP: ftp.nyse.com/Historical Data Samples/TAQ NYSE NATIONAL TRADES/
Message types in the CSV: 3 - Symbol summary record (open price, daily statistics) 34 - Trading status record (halt, resume, etc.) 220 - Trade print record (actual executed trades)
Type-220 column layout (1-indexed): 1 msg_type : Always 220 2 global_seq_num : SIP-assigned sequence number (unique within day) 3 time : HH:MM:SS.nnnnnnnnn (nanosecond precision, Eastern Time) 4 symbol : Ticker symbol (may contain spaces for test symbols) 5 exchange_code : Numeric SIP participant code for reporting exchange 6 exch_seq_num : Exchange-provided trade sequence number 7 price : Trade price (decimal; may omit leading zero, e.g., ".1185") 8 volume : Trade volume (shares) 9 sale_condition : Sale condition character(s) (e.g., "@" = regular round-lot) 10 correction : Correction indicator ("F" = final correction, " " = normal) 11 tape : Consolidated tape ("T" = Tape A/NYSE-listed, " " = other) 12 stopped_stock : Stopped stock indicator ("I" = stopped/indicated, " " = normal)
Exchange codes (field 5) — SIP participant numbering: 4 = NYSE Arca (P) 5 = NYSE (N) 6 = CBOE EDGX (K) 7 = CBOE EDGA (J) 8 = CBOE BZX (Z) 9 = CBOE BYX (Y) 10 = NYSE American (A) 11 = NASDAQ (Q) 12 = NASDAQ BX (B) 13 = IEX (V) 14 = MEMX (U) 15 = LTSE (L)

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Polygon.PolygonMarketDataClient
member.summary:
Polygon.io market data adapter implementing the IMarketDataClient abstraction. Supports full WebSocket streaming for trades, quotes, and aggregates.
Extends , which centralises connection lifecycle, resilience (retry + circuit breaker), heartbeat monitoring, and automatic reconnection. ~280 LOC of WebSocket boilerplate removed compared to the previous direct implementation.
Current support: - Trades: YES (streams "T" messages and forwards to TradeDataCollector) - Quotes: YES (streams "Q" messages and forwards to QuoteCollector) - Aggregates: YES (streams "A" and "AM" messages for second/minute bars)
Polygon WebSocket Protocol: - Endpoint: wss://socket.polygon.io/{feed} (stocks, options, forex, crypto) - Auth: Send {"action":"auth","params":"{apiKey}"} after connect - Subscribe: {"action":"subscribe","params":"T.AAPL,Q.AAPL"} - Message types: T=trade, Q=quote, A=aggregate, AM=minute aggregate
Creates a new Polygon market data client.
member.param: Event publisher for heartbeats and status.
Polygon configuration options. A valid API key is required to connect.
Unused; kept for backward-compatible constructor signature.
member.exception: If publisher, tradeCollector, or quoteCollector is null.

member.name: F:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Polygon.PolygonMarketDataClient.MinApiKeyLength
member.summary: Minimum length for a valid Polygon API key. Polygon API keys are typically 32 characters, but we accept 20+ for flexibility.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Polygon.PolygonMarketDataClient.HasValidCredentials
member.summary: Gets whether the client has a valid API key configured.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Polygon.PolygonMarketDataClient.IsConnected

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Polygon.PolygonMarketDataClient.AuthenticateAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.remarks: Polygon requires a two-step handshake before the receive loop starts: 1. Read the initial "connected" status message from the server. 2. Send the API key and read the "auth_success" response. Both reads use which reads directly from the socket before takes ownership.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Polygon.PolygonMarketDataClient.ConnectAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.remarks: Fails fast when Polygon credentials are missing so production code never falls back to synthetic data generation. Test-only stub behavior lives in the test project.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Robinhood.RobinhoodBrokerageGateway
member.summary:
Robinhood brokerage gateway for live order execution via the unofficial Robinhood API. Uses REST for order management and a bounded channel for execution report streaming.
Important: Robinhood does not publish an official public API. This gateway targets the same endpoints used by the Robinhood mobile application. Set ROBINHOOD_ACCESS_TOKEN to your personal access token.
Order types supported: market, limit, stop, stop-limit. Order modification is implemented as cancel + resubmit (Robinhood does not support in-place order modification via the unofficial API).

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Robinhood.RobinhoodBrokerageGateway.ModifyOrderAsync(System.String,Meridian.Execution.Sdk.OrderModification,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.remarks: Robinhood does not support in-place order modification via the unofficial API. This method cancels the existing order and submits a new one with the updated parameters.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Robinhood.RobinhoodHistoricalDataProvider
member.summary:
Historical data provider using the Robinhood unofficial API. Provides daily OHLCV bars for US equities via Bearer-token authentication.
Important: Robinhood does not publish an official public API. This provider targets the same endpoints used by the Robinhood mobile application. Users must supply a personal access token via the ROBINHOOD_ACCESS_TOKEN environment variable. Tokens can be obtained through the Robinhood OAuth2 login flow or from a valid browser/app session.
Rate limit: the endpoint is undocumented; the provider defaults to 100 req/hour to avoid triggering abuse-detection heuristics. Adjust RateLimitPerHour in config if needed.
Coverage: US equities and ETFs traded on Robinhood's platform.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Robinhood.RobinhoodHistoricalDataProvider.Capabilities
member.summary: Capabilities: daily bars only, US equities.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Robinhood.RobinhoodHistoricalDataProvider.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32,System.Net.Http.HttpClient,Serilog.ILogger,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new Robinhood historical data provider.
member.param: Bearer access token (falls back to ROBINHOOD_ACCESS_TOKEN env var).
Priority in fallback chain (lower = tried first, default: 35).
Whether to enable Polly retry/circuit-breaker pipeline (default: true). Pass false in unit tests to avoid retry delays.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Robinhood.RobinhoodMarketDataClient
member.summary:
Polling-based market data client using the Robinhood unofficial API. Provides real-time BBO quotes for US equities via polling the quotes endpoint.
Important: Robinhood does not provide a public WebSocket API. This client uses REST polling (default interval: 2 seconds) as the closest available substitute for streaming quotes.
Authentication: set ROBINHOOD_ACCESS_TOKEN environment variable. Trade data and market depth are not available via the Robinhood unofficial API.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Robinhood.RobinhoodOptionsChainProvider
member.summary:
Robinhood options chain provider that retrieves option chain data via the unofficial Robinhood API.
Important: Robinhood does not publish an official public API. This provider targets the same endpoints used by the Robinhood mobile application. Set ROBINHOOD_ACCESS_TOKEN to your personal access token.
API flow:
Resolve the equity instrument ID from /instruments/?symbol=…
Fetch option chain metadata from /options/chains/?equity_instrument_ids=…
Enumerate expiration dates and contracts from /options/instruments/?chain_id=…&expiration_date=…
Fetch market data from /marketdata/options/?instruments=…

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.StockSharp.StockSharpConnectorCapabilities
member.summary: Documents the capabilities of each StockSharp connector type. Provides programmatic access to what data types and features each connector supports.
member.remarks:
This class serves as a centralized reference for connector capabilities, enabling: - UI display of available features per connector - Validation of requested operations against connector capabilities - Documentation of supported data types
Capability information is based on StockSharp connector documentation and testing.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.StockSharp.StockSharpHistoricalDataProvider
member.summary: Historical data provider that leverages StockSharp connector capabilities. Enables historical data downloads through connectors like Rithmic, IQFeed, CQG, and Interactive Brokers that natively support historical data requests.
member.remarks:
This provider uses the existing configured S# connector's historical data capabilities, eliminating the need for separate backfill provider subscriptions when the streaming connector already supports historical data.
Supported connectors with historical data: - IQFeed: Full historical bars, trades, quotes - Rithmic: Historical bars and trades - CQG: Historical bars - Interactive Brokers: Historical bars
Requirements: - StockSharp must be enabled in configuration (StockSharp:Enabled = true) - EnableHistorical must be true (default) - The connector must support historical data

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.StockSharp.StockSharpMarketDataClient
member.summary:
IMarketDataClient implementation that wraps StockSharp connectors. Provides unified access to 90+ data sources through the S# adapter pattern.
Features (inspired by Hydra best practices): - Automatic reconnection with exponential backoff - Subscription recovery after reconnection - Connection health monitoring with heartbeats - Message buffering for high-frequency data
Supported data types: - Trades (tick-by-tick) - Market Depth (Level 2 order books) - Quotes (BBO)
See StockSharpConnectorFactory for available connector types.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.StockSharp.StockSharpSymbolSearchProvider
member.summary: Symbol search provider that leverages StockSharp connector's SecurityLookup capability. Enables symbol discovery through connectors like IQFeed, Interactive Brokers, and others that support native security lookup.
member.remarks:
This provider uses the existing configured S# connector's security lookup capabilities, providing unified symbol search through the same connection used for streaming data.
Supported connectors with security lookup: - IQFeed: Full symbol search across US equities, options, futures - Interactive Brokers: Global multi-asset symbol search - CQG: Futures and options symbol search - Rithmic: Futures symbol search
Requirements: - StockSharp must be enabled in configuration (StockSharp:Enabled = true) - The connector must support SecurityLookupMessage

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Adapters.Templates.TemplateBrokerageGateway
member.summary:
Template brokerage gateway scaffold. Copy this file and fill in the TODOs to implement a new brokerage provider.
Steps to implement a new brokerage: 1. Copy this file to a new folder under Adapters/{BrokerName}/ 2. Rename the class to {BrokerName}BrokerageGateway 3. Add [DataSource] and [ImplementsAdr] attributes 4. Implement all TODO methods with broker-specific API calls 5. Add JSON DTOs and a JsonSerializerContext for ADR-014 compliance 6. Register in DI: services.AddBrokerageGateway<{BrokerName}BrokerageGateway>() 7. Add configuration options record if needed 8. Add tests under tests/Meridian.Tests/Brokerage/

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.HttpClientNames
member.summary: Named HttpClient identifiers for IHttpClientFactory. Using constants ensures consistency across the codebase.
member.remarks: Implements TD-10: Replace instance HttpClient with IHttpClientFactory.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.HttpClientConfiguration
member.summary: Extension methods for configuring HttpClient instances via IHttpClientFactory.
member.remarks:
Implements TD-10: Replace instance HttpClient with IHttpClientFactory. Benefits: - Proper connection pooling and DNS refresh - Prevents socket exhaustion - Centralized configuration for timeouts, headers, retry policies - Better testability through DI
Resilience policies (retry, circuit breaker) are provided by SharedResiliencePolicies to eliminate duplication and ensure consistent behavior across all HttpClients.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.HttpClientConfiguration.AddMarketDataHttpClients(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Registers all named HttpClient configurations with the DI container.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.HttpClientConfiguration.AddMarketDataHttpClientsTracked(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Action{System.String,System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Registers all named HttpClient configurations with circuit breaker state-change reporting. Each HTTP circuit breaker calls when it transitions state, enabling the CircuitBreakerStatusService to surface breaker health in the dashboard.
member.param: Callback invoked on every circuit breaker state transition. Parameters: (breakerName, newState "Open"|"Closed"|"HalfOpen", lastError or null).

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.HttpClientFactoryProvider
member.summary: Static factory for creating HttpClient instances from IHttpClientFactory. Provides backward-compatible static access for services not yet fully converted to DI.
member.remarks:
This is a transitional pattern. New code should inject IHttpClientFactory directly.
Thread Safety: This class uses static fields and is designed to be thread-safe for the common initialization pattern where Initialize() is called once during startup.
Disposal Handling: If the underlying IServiceProvider is disposed (e.g., during test cleanup), CreateClient() will catch ObjectDisposedException and fall back to creating a new HttpClient. This ensures graceful degradation and test isolation.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.HttpClientFactoryProvider.Initialize(System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Initializes the provider with the service provider. Call this during application startup after ConfigureServices.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.HttpClientFactoryProvider.CreateClient(System.String)
member.summary: Gets an HttpClient for the specified named client. Falls back to creating a new HttpClient if factory is not initialized or disposed.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.HttpClientFactoryProvider.CreateClient(System.String,System.Action{System.Net.Http.HttpClient})
member.summary: Gets an HttpClient for the specified named client with header configuration.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.HttpClientFactoryProvider.IsInitialized
member.summary: Checks if the factory has been initialized.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.HttpClientFactoryProvider.Reset
member.summary: Resets the provider, clearing any cached factory or service provider references. Primarily for test scenarios where the service provider gets disposed between tests.

member.summary: Shared resilience policies for HTTP clients to eliminate duplicate policy definitions. Provides consistent retry and circuit breaker policies across all projects.

member.summary: Default timeout for HTTP requests.

member.name: F:Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.SharedResiliencePolicies.ShortTimeout
member.summary: Short timeout for quick operations like health checks.

member.name: F:Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.SharedResiliencePolicies.LongTimeout
member.summary: Long timeout for batch operations like backfill.

member.summary: Adds standard resilience policies (retry with exponential backoff, circuit breaker) to an HttpClient builder.

member.summary: Adds custom resilience policies with configurable parameters.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Resilience.WebSocketConnectionConfig
member.summary: Centralized configuration for WebSocket connections. Provides sensible defaults used by all streaming providers (Alpaca, Polygon, etc.) to eliminate duplicate configuration across providers.

item.description: Use for providers with fast reconnection (Alpaca, Polygon). 5 retries with 2s base delay provides ~62s total retry time. Suitable for providers with reliable infrastructure.

Use for providers with slower reconnection or on unreliable networks (StockSharp, IB Gateway). 10 retries with 3s base delay and longer circuit breaker duration. Suitable when provider infrastructure is slower or network is unstable.
Use for tick-by-tick data where latency is critical. Shorter timeouts (15s) ensure faster failure detection. Trade-off: May disconnect more frequently on slow networks.

list.item: Interactive Brokers: Resilient (TWS/Gateway may have delays)

member.summary: Maximum number of connection retry attempts.

member.summary: Base delay for exponential backoff between retries.

member.summary: Maximum delay between retries (caps exponential backoff).

member.summary: Maximum total size (bytes) of a single WebSocket message after all frames are assembled. Messages exceeding this limit are discarded and a warning is logged. Default is 10 MiB — large enough for any normal market data payload.

member.summary: Default configuration used by all streaming providers. These values match the previously duplicated settings in Alpaca and Polygon clients.

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Resilience.WebSocketConnectionConfig.HighFrequency
member.summary: Configuration optimized for high-frequency data (shorter timeouts).

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Resilience.WebSocketConnectionConfig.Resilient
member.summary: Configuration optimized for unreliable networks (more retries, longer timeouts).

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Resilience.WebSocketConnectionManager
member.summary:
Centralized WebSocket connection manager that handles connection lifecycle, resilience, heartbeat monitoring, and automatic reconnection.
This class eliminates duplicate connection management code across providers (Alpaca, Polygon, StockSharp) by centralizing: - Connection with resilience pipeline (retry + circuit breaker) - Heartbeat monitoring for stale connection detection - Reconnection gating (prevents reconnection storms) - Clean disposal and state management
member.remarks: Based on patterns from DataSourceBase, Marfusios/websocket-client, and production WebSocket implementations.

member.name: E:Meridian.Infrastructure.Resilience.WebSocketConnectionManager.ConnectionLost
member.summary: Event raised when connection is lost (heartbeat timeout or WebSocket close). Subscribers should handle reconnection logic if desired.

member.name: E:Meridian.Infrastructure.Resilience.WebSocketConnectionManager.Reconnected
member.summary: Event raised after a successful reconnection (including any onReconnected callback). Subscribers can use this for monitoring/logging reconnection events. The int parameter is the number of reconnect attempts it took.

member.name: E:Meridian.Infrastructure.Resilience.WebSocketConnectionManager.GapDetected
member.summary: Event raised when a reconnection gap is detected, providing the time window during which data may have been missed. Subscribers should trigger gap backfill for all active subscriptions covering this time range.

member.name: E:Meridian.Infrastructure.Resilience.WebSocketConnectionManager.StateChanged
member.summary: Event raised when connection state changes.

member.summary: Gets the current WebSocket state.

member.summary: Reads a single text message from the WebSocket. Must only be called after and before — it reads directly from the socket for initial handshake sequences (e.g., Polygon "connected" + "auth_success").

member.param: Optional logger; falls back to a static default.
A short description of the operation for log context.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Shared.WebSocketReconnectionHelper
member.summary: Standardized reconnection helper for WebSocket-based providers. Eliminates duplicated reconnection logic across Polygon (~40 LOC), NYSE (~30 LOC), and StockSharp (~35 LOC) providers by providing a single gated exponential-backoff-with-jitter reconnection algorithm.

remarks.para: This helper consolidates the divergent reconnection algorithms used by: - PolygonMarketDataClient: Manual exponential + jitter - NYSEDataSource: Linear multiply backoff - StockSharpMarketDataClient: Exponential + jitter with connector recreation

member.name: P:Meridian.Infrastructure.Shared.WebSocketReconnectionHelper.IsReconnecting
member.summary: Gets whether a reconnection attempt is currently in progress.

member.name: E:Meridian.Infrastructure.Shared.WebSocketReconnectionHelper.Reconnected
member.summary: Event raised after a successful reconnection, providing the disconnect and reconnect timestamps. Subscribers can use this to enqueue targeted backfill requests covering the disconnection gap window.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Shared.WebSocketReconnectionHelper.TryReconnectAsync(System.Func{System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.Task},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Attempts reconnection with exponential backoff and jitter. Guarantees only one reconnection attempt runs at a time via semaphore gating. On successful reconnection, raises the event with the disconnect/reconnect timestamps for gap backfill.
member.param: The async action that performs the actual reconnection (connect WebSocket, authenticate, resubscribe).
member.returns: True if reconnection succeeded; false if max attempts exhausted.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Shared.WebSocketReconnectionHelper.CalculateDelay(System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculates delay with exponential backoff and ±20% jitter.

member.name: T:Meridian.Infrastructure.Utilities.SymbolNormalization
member.summary: Centralized symbol normalization utilities to eliminate duplicate implementations across providers. Each provider was implementing NormalizeSymbol() separately.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Utilities.SymbolNormalization.Normalize(System.String)
member.summary: Standard symbol normalization: uppercase and trim whitespace. Used by: Alpaca, Polygon, Finnhub, Alpha Vantage, Yahoo Finance, Stooq, Nasdaq Data Link

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Utilities.SymbolNormalization.NormalizeForTiingo(System.String)
member.summary: Tiingo-specific normalization: replaces dots with dashes. Tiingo uses dashes instead of dots for class shares (e.g., BRK-A instead of BRK.A)

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Utilities.SymbolNormalization.NormalizeForYahoo(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Yahoo Finance-specific normalization for international symbols. Adds exchange suffix if not present (e.g., .L for London, .T for Tokyo)

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Utilities.SymbolNormalization.NormalizeForStooq(System.String)
member.summary: Stooq-specific normalization for Polish market symbols. Stooq uses lowercase symbols, replaces dots with dashes, and specific market suffixes.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Utilities.SymbolNormalization.NormalizeForNasdaqDataLink(System.String)
member.summary: Nasdaq Data Link (Quandl) specific normalization. Nasdaq Data Link expects uppercase symbols with dots replaced by underscores.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Utilities.SymbolNormalization.NormalizeForOpenFigi(System.String)
member.summary: OpenFIGI-specific normalization. OpenFIGI expects uppercase symbols without special characters.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Utilities.SymbolNormalization.IsValidSymbol(System.String)
member.summary: Validates that a symbol meets basic requirements.

member.name: M:Meridian.Infrastructure.Utilities.SymbolNormalization.NormalizeMany(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String})
member.summary: Batch normalize multiple symbols.
